subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are well-labelled with units and uncertainties, and sample calculations are transparent.
Communication of data processing (video tracking, regression analysis, error bars) is both clear and precise in most respects.
Interpretation of RÂ² and bound-lines is insightful and supports confidence in the model fit.
weaknesses: Propagation of uncertainties into averages and regression is absent.
Systematic errors (scale calibration, video frame-rate variability) are not addressed.
Omission of one data point (400 mL) lacks quantitative justification.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates data recording and processing with clarity and precision, earning band-5 in that strand. However, the consideration of uncertainties and the quality of data processing have significant omissions that limit both strands to the 3-4 band. Overall performance aligns with the upper range of the 3-4 band, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Research question is specific and well-contextualized with clear linkage to viscosity theory and a personal anecdote.
Materials, procedure and controlled-variables table are comprehensive and support reproducibility with only minor ambiguities.
Background concepts (viscosity, Stokesâ law) are clearly defined and appropriately referenced.
weaknesses: Temperature control was proposed but not implemented, leaving some ambiguity in the environmental setup.
Some methodological details (exact slow-motion frame rate, lighting conditions) are missing for full reproducibility.
Justification for choice of trial numbers and volume increments is not fully explained.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research design excels in stating a precise question within a specific, appropriate context and provides thorough materials and procedure detail. The methodology description allows reproduction with only minor queries, placing it at the top of the 3-4 band and just into the 5-6 band for overall quality. Minor omissions (temperature control implementation, video settings) prevent full band-6 attainment, warranting a score of 5.

strengths: The conclusion correctly describes the inverse relationship between honey concentration and terminal velocity and aligns qualitatively with the data trend.
Relevant scientific context (Stokesâ law, viscosity-velocity relation) is invoked to support the interpretation.
The trend discussion is coherent and directly tied to the experimental observations.
weaknesses: The outlier is ignored without discussion, reducing consistency with analysis.
No quantitative or numerical comparison to theoretical values is provided.
Asymptotic behavior and theoretical intercepts are not analyzed numerically.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion and context comparison are clearly stated and relevant, fitting solidly in the 3-4 band. While the student makes a sound qualitative link to Stokesâ law, the absence of numerical comparison and outlier treatment prevents full consistency in the top band. A mid-band score of 4 appropriately reflects this level of performance.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (temperature fluctuations, coarse concentration increments, release force) are identified.
Improvements proposed (temperature-controlled bath, finer intervals, inclined-plane release) are realistic and relevant.
One innovative suggestion (inclined-plane release) demonstrates depth of thought in reducing systematic error.
weaknesses: Relative impact of each identified weakness is not quantitatively explained.
Descriptions of how proposed improvements would improve precision/accuracy lack numerical justification.
No estimate of magnitude of uncertainty reduction through suggested changes.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student describes specific methodological limitations and realistic improvements, situating the work in the 3-4 band. However, the explanations of impact remain brief without quantitative evaluation, preventing the higher band. A score of 4 reflects solid descriptive evaluation with room for deeper impact analysis.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states a clear, focused research question and embeds it in a well-defined scientific and personal context.  Viscosity, Stokesâ law and buoyancy are briefly explained and directly linked to the chosen honey-water mixtures and the baking anecdote, making the context specific rather than generic.  Although the background section could be more concise, all information needed to appreciate the relevance of the question is present and appropriately referenced.

Instrument (Â±2.5 mL, Â±0.0005 m sâ»Â¹) and half-range uncertainties are identified; error bars are plotted and briefly interpreted.  However, propagation of uncertainty into the averages or the regression is absent, and systematic sources (calibration of scale, video frame rate) are not addressed.  These omissions place the work in the middle band.

Relevant considerations (multiple trials, instrument uncertainties, controlled variables, choice of volumes) are described, but they are not fully explained in terms of how they guarantee that the data will be sufficient to answer the question.  For example, the student does not justify why a 5 cm sphere or five trials are adequate, nor do they discuss the limitation of excluding viscosity or density measurements.  The treatment therefore matches the âdescribedâ level, earning the lower mark in the 3-4 band.

Specific weaknesses such as temperature fluctuations, limited increments and possible force on release are identified and briefly linked to the anomalous data, but the relative impact of each issue is not explored in depth (e.g. no estimate of percentage error).  This meets the descriptor for âdescribesâ rather than âexplainsâ.

Materials, set-up photographs, step-by-step instructions, number of trials and details of video analysis are given, so another student could reproduce the investigation with only minor queries (e.g. exact slow-motion frame rate, lighting).  Controlled variables are tabulated, but temperature control is only proposed, not implemented, leaving some ambiguity; hence the description sits comfortably in the 3-4 band rather than the top band.

Averages, half-range uncertainties and a quadratic regression are calculated correctly, and velocity is derived from video slopes.  Nevertheless, the student discards the 400 mL data point without quantitative justification and never converts honey volume to viscosity or density, limiting the depth of processing.  These significant omissions keep the strand in the 3-4 band with a mid-level mark.

Improvementsâtemperature-controlled bath, increased trials, finer concentration intervals, mechanical stirringâare realistic and relevant to the stated weaknesses, but the explanations are brief and lack discussion of how much accuracy or precision would be gained.  Therefore the strand earns the lower mark within the 3-4 band.

Raw and processed data tables include titles, units and absolute uncertainties; graphs are labelled and annotated; sample calculations are shown.  The information is laid out logically and is easy to follow, demonstrating both clarity and reasonable precision.  Minor issues (inconsistent significant figures in tables) prevent a 6 but still satisfy the top band.

Stokesâ law and the inverse relation between viscosity and terminal velocity are invoked and qualitatively discussed.  The comparison is relevant but remains descriptive; the student does not calculate theoretical terminal velocities or viscosities to make a justified, quantitative comparison.  Hence the work fits solidly in the 3-4 band.

The conclusion (velocity decreases as honey concentration increases) aligns with the processed data that the student elects to include, and the trend is described.  However, ignoring the outlier weakens full consistency, and no numerical comparison (e.g. gradient expected from Stokesâ law) is supplied.  Thus the strand satisfies âdescribed but not fully consistentâ.

annotations.comment: The introductory definition of viscosity is clear and supported by appropriate citations, establishing a solid conceptual foundation.
Consider formatting the Stokesâ law equation using LaTeX (e.g., $$F=6\pi r\eta v$$) and briefly explaining its derivation to deepen theoretical context.
The personal anecdote linking honey cake baking to the investigation context is engaging and makes the research question meaningful.
The research question is precise, quantifying honey-to-water ratios and specifying the sphereâs dimensions and terminal velocity outcome.
Specify the slow-motion frame rate (e.g., 120 fps) and lighting conditions to ensure the video-recording methodology is fully reproducible.
Link the statement on density changes explicitly to the buoyant force by referencing Archimedesâ principle in this context.
The uncertainty justification for volume measurements is valid; consider extending this to propagate through to combined calculations later.
The controlled-variables table thoroughly identifies key factors influencing viscosity and force; well organized for reproducibility.
Clarify how 20 Â°C is maintained in the âcontrolled environmentâ, as temperature control was proposed but not implemented.
The materials list is comprehensive, ensuring others can source identical equipment for replication.
Step-by-step procedure is clearly laid out, facilitating reproducibility of the experimental protocol.
Specify the cameraâs slow-motion frame rate (e.g., 120 fps) in step 2 to remove ambiguity in data capture.
The Capstone video-tracking instructions are lucid and detailed, supporting clear data processing.
The statement on the precision of video tracking is clear and aptly justifies using positionâtime graphs.
The anomalous high velocity in the 400 ml honey trial should be examined â indicate whether it stems from data entry error or experimental variance.
In the raw data table, ensure consistent significant figures across all velocity values for precision.
Sample calculation of average velocity is correct and well documented, aiding transparency.
Consider including uncertainty propagation when averaging the velocities to reflect combined measurement errors.
The interpretation of the high RÂ² value demonstrates strong model fit; this clarity is well communicated.
Linking the asymptotic behavior to Stokesâ law intercept values would strengthen the theoretical justification.
Explanation of bound-lines impact is clear and supports confidence in the regression model.
Clarify how squaring the x-axis in Graph 2 confirms the quadratic relation rather than distorting data distribution.
The scientific context links observations to Stokesâ law well; include a numerical theoretical comparison to strengthen justification.
Evaluating how much temperature fluctuations degrade viscosity measurement (e.g., Â±1 Â°C impact) would quantify this methodological weakness.
Explain quantitatively how a temperature-controlled bath would reduce viscosity uncertainty (e.g., variation in PaÂ·s).
Proposing an inclined-plane release is innovative and could effectively eliminate applied force error.
Specify a standard waiting time between mixing and dropping the sphere to control settling effects.
The extension proposing to vary honey temperature is well connected to viscosity studies and offers valuable further inquiry.
The error-bar discussion is insightful in assessing significance, showing clear understanding of uncertainty interpretation.
Explain why 100 ml increments were chosen rather than smaller steps, in terms of resolution necessary for trend detection.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
